summary.headline: Offline A/B experiment ready for executive review
summary.text: The offline A/B Experiment sample uses the same objective, audience, platform mix, and criteria for both variants. Variant A is the speed-led clarity hypothesis; Variant B is the trust-proof hypothesis. No winner is selected from synthetic evidence; use this as a planning prompt for human review, not a forecast.

cards.title: Overall Reaction
cards.detail: Aggregate synthetic response for Variant A baseline; compare directionally against Variant B.

cards.title: Message Clarity
cards.detail: Use the same Campaign Message Test card pattern for A/B review.

cards.title: Tone Fit
cards.detail: Review tone and proof before external use.

cards.title: Claim Readiness
cards.detail: Claims in both variants require human evidence review before launch approval.

cards.title: Platform Fit
cards.detail: Start with a small approved channel test, not broad scaling.

comparisonCards.title: Variant A Clarity Comparison
comparisonCards.value: Variant A is the clarity hypothesis
comparisonCards.detail: Same criterion for both variants: speed-led clarity versus trust-proof clarity; no winner is selected from synthetic evidence.

comparisonCards.title: Variant B Trust Comparison
comparisonCards.value: Variant B is the trust-proof hypothesis
comparisonCards.detail: Same criterion for both variants: trust proof and reassurance; Variant B provides the clearer trust rationale for the next test.

comparisonCards.title: Decision Status
comparisonCards.detail: Both variants require comparable field evidence; current output supports human-reviewed next-test planning only.

comparisonMethod.rationale: Same objective, audience, platform mix, context, and criteria are used for both variants; synthetic evidence is not sufficient to select a production winner.
comparisonMethod.parityCheck: Variant A and Variant B use the same audience, platforms, context, evaluation criteria, and offline evidence basis.

audienceInsights: Working Adults: Variant A emphasizes speed; Variant B adds reliability proof.
Urban Consumers: compare convenience clarity against quality reassurance.
SME Owners: keep team-benefit framing without guaranteed outcomes.
Campaign response fixture separates aggregate message comprehension from any actionable targeting or persuasion workflow.
risksCaveats: Reviewed offline sample only; not production-ready and not connected to production campaign systems.
Not suitable for persuasion optimization, voter targeting, microtargeting, or guaranteed prediction claims.
Dashboard signals require human review before executive or public-facing use.
Offline fixture only: no live social data, private data, CRM lists, or production prediction.
recommendedNextTest: Run a small approved A/B message-readiness test after human review: speed-led Variant A versus trust-proof Variant B.
safetyLabels: No PII, CRM lists, private messages, or voter lists
No microtargeting, persuasion optimization, or conversion guarantees

exports.status: Generated from the reviewed offline A/B Experiment sample; review safety notes before reuse.
exports.executiveSummaryPreview: Executive review: Variant A and Variant B are ready for directional human review; Variant A response is light positive signal and Variant B trust is light positive signal. Use this to plan a small approved A/B evidence test.

assumptions: Variant A is Healthy lunch decisions in under 10 minutes for busy urban teams.
Variant B is Team lunch made simple with reviewed nutrition cues and dependable delivery windows.
Key message is Compare speed-led clarity against trust-proof reassurance before budget review.
Claim to review is Variant claims are assumptions for review, not verified production claims.
Audience focus is Working Adults, Urban Consumers, SME Owners.
Use only aggregate, synthetic, offline assumptions with no customer records or private audience data.
message_theme: Healthy lunch decisions in under 10 minutes for busy urban teams.
The review contract was validated as offline/synthetic/demo-only before conversion.
Audience reactions are aggregate simulation signals, not measured public opinion or individual behavior.
No approved field data or backtest evidence is attached to this bridge output.
evidenceGaps: No real social platform APIs, private social scraping, private groups, CRM records, voter lists, or direct messages were used.
Treat outputs as synthetic simulation estimates unless separately validated with approved field data or backtests.
Offline sample mode uses deterministic sample data and is not evidence from the field.
limitations: Reviewed offline sample only; not production-ready and not connected to production campaign systems.
Not suitable for persuasion optimization, voter targeting, microtargeting, or guaranteed prediction claims.
Dashboard signals require human review before executive or public-facing use.
Offline workbench sample only; not a production simulator replacement.
Deterministic fixtures only by default; no live social APIs, private social data, credentials, PII, voter lists, or CRM data.
